expectations.text: States (statement -> tail-recursion) #9 is NOT available for this tree recursion (no single tail call)
expectations.evidence: LLM judge: "flatten over a tree makes multiple recursive calls per frame (one per nested array), so it's non-tail recursion. Even in an engine with TCO, tail-call elimination can't save a call that isn't in tail position. This is the crux -- you can't rescue tree recursion with a tail-call trick." -- explicitly the branching/no-tail-position structure, separate from the TCO point.

expectations.text: Recommends an explicit-stack (if -> while) iteration or a generator-as-state-machine
Coach, don't drive (no edit / no test run)
